title: rails/sample.rb has a leftover placeholder model name that reads oddly
body: The SomeClass example in rails/sample.rb has 'has_many :watermelons' alongside 'has_many :apples' and 'belongs_to :tree, class_name: "Plant"'. It reads like a placeholder that was never cleaned up and distracts from the point of the example, which is about association/validation ordering conventions.

title: swift/sample.swift guard-let example is easy to copy incorrectly
body: The multi-condition guard example in swift/sample.swift puts 'else { return }' on its own line after the second let, but doesn't call out that indentation of the continued condition matters for readability; new hires keep formatting it inconsistently when copying the pattern into real code.

title: javascript-typescript/sample.js only shows the 'good' style, no contrasting bad example
body: Unlike testing-rspec's specs which pair a '# Not recommended' block with a '# Recommended' block, javascript-typescript/sample.js only has one short snippet demonstrating object/class spacing conventions. Adding a contrasting anti-pattern would make the rule clearer for reviewers linking to this file in PR comments.

title: avoid_let_spec.rb doesn't show incremental migration from a let-heavy spec
body: testing-rspec/avoid_let_spec.rb jumps straight from a 'Not recommended' block using several `let`s to a fully rewritten 'Recommended' version with a local build_policy helper. A shorter intermediate step would help engineers migrating large legacy spec files see how to do it incrementally.

title: predicate_tests_spec.rb and unit_test_spec.rb guidance seems to overlap
body: Both testing-rspec/predicate_tests_spec.rb and testing-rspec/unit_test_spec.rb cover assertions on boolean-ish return values and it's not obvious from either file which one takes precedence when they suggest different phrasing for the same kind of test.
